page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on external storage options and network-attached resources. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.g6a.xlarge demonstrates modest performance characteristics. Its single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, falls into the lower tier of tested hardware. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests show weak throughput, with read-only large block transfers reaching 31.83 GB/sec and write operations at 16.28 GB/sec. However, the processor performs adequately in specific tasks, achieving average results in gzip compression at 23.81 GB/sec and PassMark CPU encryption tests.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a dedicated hardware profile suitable for workloads that require predictable CPU allocation without the premium cost of high-performance compute or memory-optimized classes. The lack of bundled local storage and moderate network throughput represent clear tradeoffs, positioning this instance for workloads with lower I/O demands.

The ecs.g6a.xlarge is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as basic web serving, lightweight application hosting, and moderate database operations that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage or intensive parallel processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor, it provides 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPU accelerators, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with lower-tier results in stress-ng CPU and memory bandwidth tests, though it achieves average marks in gzip compression and PassMark encryption. This instance represents a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without high-performance demands. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, basic web serving, and lightweight database operations.
